title: Grammar · English B2
scope: B2 (Cambridge First) adds the present perfect continuous, past perfect, third and mixed conditionals, wish/if only, the full passive including reporting passives, complete reported speech, non-defining relative clauses, modals of deduction, the causative have/get something done, and advanced verb patterns. Boundary: you handle nuance and hypothesis about the past; emphasis and inversion are C1.

blocks.text: have/has been + verb-ing, for an action that started in the past and continues, or has recent results. Emphasises duration.
I have been studying for three hours. It has been raining all day.
had + past participle, for an action completed before another past action. The continuous (had been + -ing) stresses duration.
When I arrived, the film had already started.
She was tired because she had been working all night.
Unreal situations in the past: If + past perfect, would have + past participle.
If I had studied more, I would have passed the exam.
Combine a past condition with a present result, or vice versa.
If I had taken that job, I would be in London now. (past condition → present result)

rows: I wish I had more time.
I wish I had studied harder.

blocks.text: The passive works in every tense, and impersonal reporting structures are common in formal English.

rows: He is said to be very rich.
It is believed that prices will rise.

blocks.text: Tense backshift, plus changes of pronouns and time/place words. Reported questions use statement word order.

rows: He said he had seen her the day before.
He told me to close the door.

blocks.text: Non-defining clauses add extra (non-essential) information and use commas; that cannot be used in them.
My brother, who lives in Spain, is a teacher. (non-defining)
The book that I bought is interesting. (defining)
must (sure it's true), can't (sure it's false), might/could (possible) — present and past.

rows: She must be tired. He can't be serious.
They must have left. She can't have known.

blocks.text: have/get + object + past participle, when someone else does the action for you.
I had my car repaired. She is getting her hair cut.
